{"Description":"","Name":"Ownward like feature","Status":"To Do","Team":"Features Dump","date:Deadline:is_datetime":0,"url":"https://app.notion.com/p/3a00f43189ea8006a2b3d31d85149c6c"}
</properties>
<content>
# Ownward
A personal management app I wanted to create for myself to replace multiple third-party app-bloat (like Notion and ChatGPT’s scheduled tasks) for project, task, and personal management.
This will now be provided as a feature in Minkops
</content>
</page>

<content>
# LeetCode Top Interview 150
<empty-block/>
Previous DSA Theory section removed. DSA will now be learnt while solving these problems, learning new concepts, data structures, and patterns just in time as they are needed.
## Math
- [x] 9. Palindrome Number
- [x] 66. Plus One
- [ ] 172. Factorial Trailing Zeroes
- [ ] 69. Sqrt(x)
- [ ] 50. Pow(x, n)
- [ ] 149. Max Points on a Line
## Array / String
- [ ] 88. Merge Sorted Array
- [ ] 27. Remove Element
- [ ] 26. Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
- [ ] 80. Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II
- [ ] 169. Majority Element
- [ ] 189. Rotate Array
- [ ] 121. Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
- [ ] 122. Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
- [ ] 55. Jump Game
- [ ] 45. Jump Game II
- [ ] 274. H-Index
- [ ] 380. Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
- [ ] 238. Product of Array Except Self
- [ ] 134. Gas Station
- [ ] 135. Candy
- [ ] 42. Trapping Rain Water
- [ ] 13. Roman to Integer
- [ ] 12. Integer to Roman
- [ ] 58. Length of Last Word
- [ ] 14. Longest Common Prefix
- [ ] 151. Reverse Words in a String
- [ ] 6. Zigzag Conversion
- [ ] 28. Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String
- [ ] 68. Text Justification
---
## Two Pointers
- [ ] 125. Valid Palindrome
- [ ] 392. Is Subsequence
- [ ] 167. Two Sum II – Input Array Is Sorted
- [ ] 11. Container With Most Water
- [ ] 15. 3Sum
---
## Sliding Window
- [ ] 209. Minimum Size Subarray Sum
- [ ] 3. Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
- [ ] 30. Substring with Concatenation of All Words
- [ ] 76. Minimum Window Substring
---
## Matrix
- [ ] 36. Valid Sudoku
- [ ] 54. Spiral Matrix
- [ ] 48. Rotate Image
- [ ] 73. Set Matrix Zeroes
- [ ] 289. Game of Life
---
## Hashmap
- [ ] 383. Ransom Note
- [ ] 205. Isomorphic Strings
- [ ] 290. Word Pattern
- [ ] 242. Valid Anagram
- [ ] 49. Group Anagrams
- [ ] 1. Two Sum
- [ ] 202. Happy Number
- [ ] 219. Contains Duplicate II
- [ ] 128. Longest Consecutive Sequence
---
## Intervals
- [ ] 228. Summary Ranges
- [ ] 56. Merge Intervals
- [ ] 57. Insert Interval
- [ ] 452. Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
---
## Stack
- [ ] 20. Valid Parentheses
- [ ] 71. Simplify Path
- [ ] 155. Min Stack
- [ ] 150. Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
- [ ] 224. Basic Calculator
---
## Linked List
- [ ] 141. Linked List Cycle
- [ ] 2. Add Two Numbers
- [ ] 21. Merge Two Sorted Lists
- [ ] 138. Copy List with Random Pointer
- [ ] 92. Reverse Linked List II
- [ ] 25. Reverse Nodes in k-Group
- [ ] 19. Remove Nth Node From End of List
- [ ] 82. Remove Duplicates from Sorted List II
- [ ] 61. Rotate List
- [ ] 86. Partition List
- [ ] 146. LRU Cache
---
## Binary Tree General
- [ ] 104. Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
- [ ] 100. Same Tree
- [ ] 226. Invert Binary Tree
- [ ] 101. Symmetric Tree
- [ ] 105. Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
- [ ] 106. Construct Binary Tree from Inorder and Postorder Traversal
- [ ] 117. Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node II
- [ ] 114. Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
- [ ] 112. Path Sum
- [ ] 129. Sum Root to Leaf Numbers
- [ ] 124. Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
- [ ] 173. Binary Search Tree Iterator
- [ ] 222. Count Complete Tree Nodes
- [ ] 236. Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
---
## Binary Tree BFS
- [ ] 199. Binary Tree Right Side View
- [ ] 637. Average of Levels in Binary Tree
- [ ] 102. Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
- [ ] 103. Binary Tree Zigzag Level Order Traversal
---
## Binary Search Tree
- [ ] 530. Minimum Absolute Difference in BST
- [ ] 230. Kth Smallest Element in a BST
- [ ] 98. Validate Binary Search Tree
---
## Graph General
- [ ] 200. Number of Islands
- [ ] 130. Surrounded Regions
- [ ] 133. Clone Graph
- [ ] 399. Evaluate Division
- [ ] 207. Course Schedule
- [ ] 210. Course Schedule II
---
## Graph BFS
- [ ] 909. Snakes and Ladders
- [ ] 433. Minimum Genetic Mutation
- [ ] 127. Word Ladder
---
## Trie
- [ ] 208. Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
- [ ] 211. Design Add and Search Words Data Structure
- [ ] 212. Word Search II
---
## Backtracking
- [ ] 17. Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
- [ ] 77. Combinations
- [ ] 46. Permutations
- [ ] 39. Combination Sum
- [ ] 52. N-Queens II
- [ ] 22. Generate Parentheses
- [ ] 79. Word Search
---
## Divide & Conquer
- [ ] 108. Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree
- [ ] 148. Sort List
- [ ] 427. Construct Quad Tree
- [ ] 23. Merge k Sorted Lists
---
## Kadane’s Algorithm
- [ ] 53. Maximum Subarray
- [ ] 918. Maximum Sum Circular Subarray
---
## Binary Search
- [ ] 35. Search Insert Position
- [ ] 74. Search a 2D Matrix
- [ ] 162. Find Peak Element
- [ ] 33. Search in Rotated Sorted Array
- [ ] 34. Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array
- [ ] 153. Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
- [ ] 4. Median of Two Sorted Arrays
---
## Heap
- [ ] 215. Kth Largest Element in an Array
- [ ] 502. IPO
- [ ] 373. Find K Pairs with Smallest Sums
- [ ] 295. Find Median from Data Stream
---
## Bit Manipulation
- [ ] 67. Add Binary
- [ ] 190. Reverse Bits
- [ ] 191. Number of 1 Bits
- [ ] 136. Single Number
- [ ] 137. Single Number II
- [ ] 201. Bitwise AND of Numbers Range
---
## 1D DP
- [ ] 70. Climbing Stairs
- [ ] 198. House Robber
- [ ] 139. Word Break
- [ ] 322. Coin Change
- [ ] 300. Longest Increasing Subsequence
---
## Multidimensional DP
- [ ] 120. Triangle

- [ ] 63. Unique Paths II
- [ ] 5. Longest Palindromic Substring
- [ ] 97. Interleaving String
- [ ] 72. Edit Distance
- [ ] 123. Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock III
- [ ] 188. Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock IV
- [ ] 221. Maximal Square
</content>

tasks.Name: Agent Platform
tasks.pageText:
Here is the result of "view" for the Page with URL https://app.notion.com/p/d250f43189ea839eb6cc81f02ea323f0 as of 2026-07-17T15:29:40.622Z:
<page url="https://app.notion.com/p/d250f43189ea839eb6cc81f02ea323f0" icon="🌐">
<ancestor-path>
<parent-data-source url="collection://90f0f431-89ea-83dc-a148-87895e93e853" name="Minkops Kanban"/>
<ancestor-2-database url="https://app.notion.com/p/3970f43189ea802ba512d56aacbb133c" title=""/>
</ancestor-path>
<properties>
{"Description":"","Name":"Agent Platform","Status":"To Do","Team":"Tasks","date:Deadline:is_datetime":0,"url":"https://app.notion.com/p/d250f43189ea839eb6cc81f02ea323f0"}
</properties>
<content>
### Read before every session
- React (Codex) + FastAPI (Handcode)
‼️Handcode just enough to be confident. Handcode at least enough to be confident. Then velocity.
‼️‼️ The biggest bottleneck is aimless roaming 
‼️‼️ **START BY PLANNING THIS SESSION, STRICTLY TIMEBOX GOALS AND AIM TO FINISH !!**
‼️ Make Unit tests and Logging a default for all projects
---
- S***ATURDAYS - Weekly verbal walkthrough of changes/major decisions and whiteboarding.***
- Whole day’s learning into Practice first, then following (Remember the goal is shipping with zero debt)
<empty-block/>
- [x] Make DATES and TITLES Consistent across Resume, LinkedIn, Company docs. Finalize SWE+DE Resume and LinkedIn
- [x] Edit the database
	- [x] Study the current seed file and fully understand SQL + Mental Map + Design Choices
	- [x] Separate init seed file and schema seed file
	- [x] Study the Human-Agent Queue notes in Obsidian
	- [x] Implement the plan
	- [x] Status `dead` alert system
- [x] Login page first or Dashboard page first? Database changes?
- [ ] Paper-Pen drafting — all features, architecture, design of the MVP
- [ ] Login Page
- [ ] Dashboard page
	- [ ] Routes
	- [ ] Connect to database to retrieve data from `tenants` table.
	- [ ] Use `curl` for testing
- [ ] Worker Process
	- [x] Watch [The Outbox Pattern is seriously underrated...](https://youtu.be/7Js-4GuNogM?si=x1Z5yX5HP4vyhBVe)
	- [ ] Deeply Understand the concept + “How to Introduce Workers” in “Event Outbox” in Obsidian
	- [ ] Implement `ai-suite/workers`
- [ ] Multi-threading/Multi-processing/Async practice - check Obsidian+more. This is important.
- [ ] Glue in `docker compose` to run 1 task end to end - a simple Imel task
	- [ ] 1. User creates a task in dashboard (either One time tasks, like create a post, or Running tasks, like check emails and respond as they arrive)
	- [ ] 2. Agents pick up the task from an outbox/queue, perform AI, and send further actions to `EventOutbox`
	- [ ] 3. `ai-suite` picks the side effect from `EventOutbox` and performs the task. As soon as it’s done, dashboard shows completed.
- [ ] Implement GitHub Actions CI/CD
	- [ ] Take care of prerequisites like App, Hosting, etc.
- [ ] Brainstorm and implement one K8s flow end-to-end
- [ ] Write (and learn) tests - production style
- [ ] [How to Design a RAG Pipeline for 10 Million Documents with Zero Hallucination ( Live Demo )](https://medium.com/@visrow/how-to-design-a-rag-pipeline-for-10-million-documents-with-zero-hallucination-live-demo-057e37bcdbf6)
<empty-block/>
</content>
</page>

-
- [ ] Documents

- [ ] a **public technical case study** that gives enough implementation detail without leaking private code. This can include architecture diagrams, schema snippets, sample dbt model names, synthetic SQL examples, anonymized dashboard screenshots, pipeline descriptions, data quality checks, and design trade-offs.
- [ ] Prepare all four ventures for final credibility
	- [ ] Business Address
	- [ ] Register Business
	- [ ] Setup Professional-grade final Website for MH
	- [ ] Setup Production-grade final website for Minkops

- [All Data Engineering Interviews Explained!](https://youtu.be/jdrwZfeTd-o?si=SAHADaOCd0fCvgmF)
<page url="https://app.notion.com/p/8260f43189ea826ca698019783091345">Job Search Sites</page>
- Find Hiring manager’s name, then cold email them on company email - Either find the domain of other easy-to-target person working there, or guess the domain.
- LinkedIn Experience: First, Data Engineer building foundations of MH \> Then, Lead AI Engineer building MinkOps

	- AngelList

- MOCK INTERVIEWS

	- Ruthlessly practice the SAR Schema
	- Prepare re-usable stories, scenarios, case studies with **constraints, deadlines, performance **focus. Don’t tell features, tell problems and solutions.

<content>
<empty-block/>
**‼️Every check-list item should end in a verifiable output, not just code.**
**‼️Each check-list item is to time-boxed for 1 hour. Attempt to finish in one hour.**
<empty-block/>
- [x] Draft an MVP Architecture
- [x] Define Persona and Policy
- [x] Define Nodes
- [x] Summarize Imel - How and Why everything works - Generalize the flow for a general agent
- [x] Refactor as per “Thinking in Langgraph” (Command\[\] etc) - no use of AI
- [x] Code the Graph
- [x] Design temporary Database in Postgres after Data Modelling Masterclass
	- [ ] Either for MH, or
	- [x] Minkops
- [x] Boilerplate INSERT INTO DB + Understand the last two parts of “Move from DB to DWH”.
- [x] Create a Vector Database using pgvector for MH. Watch the Vector DB video in the 01-[Vector Database](/p/b8b0f43189ea8316bf3401af50aaa591?pvs=25) page.
	- [x] For each tenant, divide between a relational data part, a file/S3 part, and a vector/embedding part (no need to be disjoint); and decide new tables/schema to create/modify
- [x] Modify the `company_kb_lookup` node to use a vector store
- [x] Read and understand and summarize all the vector store related changes (ALL CODE)
- [x] ⭐️ System Design Refactoring (NEEDS HIGH-FOCUS ZONES)
	- [x] Redefine Nodes on the graph
	- [x] Summarize layers manually (Separate agent-specs vs shared capabilities from existing Imel)
	- [x] Read the Refactoring Guide in Obsidian
	- [x] Read Refactoring guide and cleanup `tools.py\``
	- [x] Refactor all code to separate [Shared Capabilities](/p/df90f43189ea827fb30381ebe02dd4d4?pvs=25) (Manually first)
	- [x] Implement Langgraph’s `Command` instead of poor `route_by_intent_node`
	- [x] Study last Anti-gravity chat and log into Obsidian
	- [x] Thoroughly study the new architecture, and create a detailed documentable architecture diagram (one flowchart, and one layers diagram)
</content>
</page>

# SQL 50 + Advanced SQL 50 
‼️ Learning SQL and relevant quick start has to happen WITH problems! No separate theory first sections.
## Part 2 — SQL Foundations: Writing Correct and Idiomatic Queries
### Easy
- [x] \[S\] [1757 — Recyclable and Low Fat Products](https://leetcode.com/problems/recyclable-and-low-fat-products/)

tasks.Name: Live Engineering
tasks.pageText:
Here is the result of "view" for the Page with URL https://app.notion.com/p/ee10f43189ea82599dd38120ec89d012 as of 2026-07-18T04:32:17.873Z: {"Description":"","Name":"Live Engineering","Status":"In Progress","Team":"Tutorials","date:Deadline:end":"2026-09-02","date:Deadline:is_datetime":0,"date:Deadline:start":"2026-07-17","url":"https://app.notion.com/p/ee10f43189ea82599dd38120ec89d012"} The following is a list of implementations/projects on Myndral and Minkops.
No agentic coding — Claude teaches, I handcode. Each of the following build on top of previous, and learn just-in-time while doing. Each of these are broad tasks with huge scope, so they should span across multiple days. Prioritize depth and quality over deadlines. - [ ] An industry standard [DWH on Myndral.](/p/3a10f43189ea80a1813dc57008f5f7d0?pvs=25) - [ ] Implementing CI/CD on Myndral - [ ] Learning CI/CD from the current implementation - [ ] Moving the DWH, etc. to production GCP - [ ] Learning GCP using the current implementation - [ ] IaaC GCP using Terraform and gcloud - [ ] All of the above for Minkops - [ ] A [RAG+Agentic Project](/p/2b80f43189ea8204974e814d3dc65cdc?pvs=25) — enter Minkops - [ ] Uses MCP - [ ] Uses RAG - [ ] Uses Agent orchestration - [ ] Uses MLOps - [ ] A Kafka Project - [ ] A Pyspark Project - [ ] Load Testing - [ ] Video Processing Pipeline - [ ] Distributed Job Queue - [ ] Repeat all above on Minkops.

tasks.Name: dbt
tasks.pageText:
Here is the result of "view" for the Page with URL https://app.notion.com/p/41d0f43189ea8226811d81da356d003d as of 2026-07-08T07:30:36.363Z:
<page url="https://app.notion.com/p/41d0f43189ea8226811d81da356d003d" icon="📄">
<ancestor-path>
<parent-data-source url="collection://90f0f431-89ea-83dc-a148-87895e93e853" name="Minkops Kanban"/>
<ancestor-2-database url="https://app.notion.com/p/3970f43189ea802ba512d56aacbb133c" title=""/>
</ancestor-path>
<properties>
{"Description":"","Name":"dbt","Status":"Done","Team":"Tutorials","date:Deadline:end":"2026-03-22","date:Deadline:is_datetime":0,"date:Deadline:start":"2026-03-20","url":"https://app.notion.com/p/41d0f43189ea8226811d81da356d003d"}
</properties>
<content>
‼️ *Unlike System Design, DSA and Postgres (partial), majority of practice of dbt and all other DE topics happens directly on Minkops, be aggressive, and define Minkops daily task based on what’s studied on that day, instead of the other way round.*
<empty-block/>
Practice `dbt` on Minkops 
‼️ `dbt` is just a tool. In this section, give serious depth to DWH concepts over `dbt`
## Data Warehousing
- [x] [Data Modeling Layer & Concepts \| The Analytics Setup Guidebook](https://www.holistics.io/books/setup-analytics/data-modeling-layer-and-concepts/) \[FULL BOOK\]
- [x] [Slowly changing dimension](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slowly_changing_dimension)
- [x] [Apache Parquet: Efficient Data Storage \| Databricks](https://www.databricks.com/glossary/what-is-parquet)
- [x] [Row vs Column Oriented Databases](https://dataschool.com/data-modeling-101/row-vs-column-oriented-databases/)
- [x] [Star Schema vs. OBT for Data Warehouse Performance \| Blog \| Fivetran](https://www.fivetran.com/blog/star-schema-vs-obt)
- [x] Ansh Lamba Tutorials
	- [x] Pre-requisite: [Data Generating Sources](/p/f790f43189ea83f79283015ba6bd3091?pvs=25) \[START HERE - NOT OPTIONAL NOW\]
	- [x] [\[Data Build Tool\] DBT - The Ultimate Guide \| With CI/CD](https://youtu.be/B8uwFmVt4sU?si=iJKOeIGRKeLZgdtJ) 
	- [x] [Airbnb End-To-End Data Engineering Project (For Beginners) \| DBT + Snowflake + AWS](https://youtu.be/3SZSDKEZqoA?si=cjLxISG_4y9O5fzh) - Do his project
### Use Claude to understand both Theory then Practice for the following
- [ ] dbt Test
- [x] dbt Seeds
- [x] dbt Macros and Jinjas
- [x] dbt Snapshots for SCD
- [ ] Partitioning (in DB vs DWH)
- [ ] Micropartitions / Immutable files
- [ ] Materialized views & Incremental view maintenance
- [ ] CQRS
- [ ] Event sourcing
- [ ] CDC
- [ ] How do you separate compute and storage etc? (in Postgres Obsidian)
- [ ] Functional Data Engineering Paradigm
- [x] Understanding Idempotency with a Minkops task
- [ ] Applying Backfills in a Minkops task
- [ ] Event Driven Pipelines and other kinds of pipelines 
- [ ] As many real-world use cases/case studies of Data Models (e.g. e-commerce, streaming service, etc) as you can
- [ ] Study as many Reference Architectures (AWS reference architecture section) as possible
</content>
</page>
